## Items for Human Review

| # | Column / Section | Confidence | Question |
|---|-----------------|------------|----------|
| 1 | Email, TaxCode, TaxCode2, TaxCode3 | High | **PII concern** — table contains email addresses and tax identification numbers. Confirm access controls are appropriate and these columns should be included in any UC migration. |
| 2 | Insert_date source | Medium | Mapped from `base_pop.update_date` in the Google Sheet. Confirm this represents the date the CID was added to the sheet vs. the date the sheet row was last modified. |
| 3 | Google Sheets dependency | Medium | Base population depends on `External_Fivetran_gsheets_tin_gep_temp_pop` — an external Google Sheet synced via Fivetran. Confirm: (a) who maintains this sheet, (b) what the Fivetran sync frequency is, (c) whether this is intended to be a permanent or truly temporary data source. |
| 4 | Commented-out filters | Medium | SP_TIN_Gap_Temp_pop has all Freeze6 customer-level filters commented out (FTD, PlayerStatus, AccountStatus, VerificationLevel, Regulation, AccountType, CountryID, PlayerLevel). Confirm this is intentional — no eligibility filtering means any CID in the Google Sheet will appear regardless of account state. |
| 5 | [Group] char(1) vs. char(2) | Low | BI_DB_TIN_Gap uses char(2) for Group (A/B1/B2/B3/C). This table uses char(1) (A/B/C). Confirm the simplified grouping is intentional and downstream consumers handle both formats. |
| 6 | No #trading_activity temp table | Low | Unlike BI_DB_TIN_Gap, there is no Active_Ind check derived from trading activity. Group assignment uses only PlayerLevelID, equity, and position count. Confirm this simplification is acceptable. |
| 7 | LastLoggedIn source difference | Low | LastLoggedIn is sourced from BI_DB_CIDFirstDates instead of Fact_CustomerAction (used by BI_DB_TIN_Gap). These may return different dates — confirm which is preferred for this population. |
| 8 | "Temp" longevity | Low | Table name suggests temporary use, but it has a regular daily TRUNCATE+INSERT pattern. Confirm whether this table is still needed or should be retired. |
